preprocessing: Import pandas, numpy, and scikit-learn preprocessing libraries.
Read the CSV file from ./data/raw/ibmchurn.csv into a dataframe.
Drop the column 'customerID' from the dataframe as it is an identifier.
Convert 'TotalCharges' from object to numeric; coerce errors and fill resulting NaN values with 0.
Convert all boolean-like columns ('SeniorCitizen', 'Partner', 'Dependents', 'PhoneService', 'PaperlessBilling', 'Churn') to binary numeric format (1/0).
Encode binary categorical columns like 'gender' to numerical (1/0).
Impute any remaining missing values in the dataset with the appropriate strategy (if any remain after previous steps).

Apply one-hot encoding for all categorical columns with more than two categories ('MultipleLines', 'InternetService', 'OnlineSecurity', 'OnlineBackup', 'DeviceProtection', 'TechSupport', 'StreamingTV', 'StreamingMovies', 'Contract', 'PaymentMethod').
Address class imbalance in target 'Churn' by applying SMOTE or a suitable resampling method.
training: Import scikit-learn model selection and LogisticRegression libraries.
Split the data into features and target column 'Churn'.
Split the data into training and validation sets using stratified sampling.
Initialize a Logistic Regression model with appropriate parameters for class imbalance (e.g., class_weight='balanced').
Fit the Logistic Regression model to the training data.
Save the trained Logistic Regression model to ./models/logreg_ibmchurn.pkl.
